description:
## What
At update_credentials time, render the raw bio (markdown or plain text) through pulldown-cmark to produce HTML, then sanitize the HTML through ammonia using its default tag/attribute allowlist. Store rendered HTML in bio_html column. Ensure no XSS vectors survive. Specifically test script injection, javascript: href, data: URI, and event handler attributes.

## Why
Bios are rendered as HTML in clients. Unsanitized HTML is an XSS vector. pulldown-cmark + ammonia is the correct pipeline: render markdown to HTML, then strip unsafe tags/attrs. Part of sub-epic 8-D.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read ammonia crate README to document the default allowed tags and attributes (the exact allowlist is the oracle); check pulldown-cmark's handling of raw HTML blocks in markdown input; verify that ammonia strips script tags, on* event attributes, javascript: href, data: href, and style attributes by default.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add pulldown-cmark and ammonia as dependencies if not already present; implement render_bio(input: &str) -> String function that runs input through pulldown-cmark::html::push_html then ammonia::clean; store result in accounts.bio_html; return bio_html in the Account.note field (not the raw markdown); also return raw markdown in the source field if Mastodon does (check mastodon.source.note field).
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write unit tests for render_bio with the following independent-oracle test vectors: input scalpel test vectors from the ammonia crate's own test suite (not from the code under test); test: plain text roundtrips; markdown bold/italic renders; script tag stripped; javascript: href stripped; data: href stripped; onerror= attribute stripped; style= attribute stripped; img src remains for allowed domains; relative links not mangled.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: ammonia crate's own test suite test vectors (github.com/nickel-lang/ammonia/tests); these are authoritative for what ammonia strips and allows. Do NOT derive expected outputs by running ammonia in the test — compute expected HTML by reading the ammonia allowlist spec.
- Test approach: pure unit tests (no DB needed); render_bio is a pure function; test each XSS vector independently; for markdown rendering, use pulldown-cmark directly in the test as the oracle (both the test and implementation use same crate — acceptable here because the oracle is the spec not the impl).

## Defensive programming checklist
- Use ammonia::clean (default allowlist) not ammonia::clean_text (which strips all HTML)
- Never store unsanitized HTML in bio_html
- bio_html column is NOT NULL; if bio is empty string, store empty string not NULL
- pulldown-cmark raw HTML pass-through: if input contains raw HTML blocks, they are sanitized by ammonia afterward — this is correct behavior
- No .unwrap() anywhere
- render_bio must not panic on any Unicode input including emoji, RTL text, or null bytes (strip null bytes before processing)
title: Persona bio HTML rendering — pulldown-cmark + ammonia XSS sanitization
